web_results.title: Trump ‘not happy’ with Iran situation and says military force is still an option | Iran | The Guardian
web_results.description: The remarks came at the White House on Friday after talks between the US and Iran on Tehran’s nuclear programme ended inconclusively, with a suggestion that further discussions would be held next week. Trump told reporters he favoured diplomacy but repeated his insistence that Iran could not possess a nuclear weapon. “It’d be wonderful if they negotiated in … good faith and conscience but they are not getting there so far,” he said. Asked whether US strikes could lead to regime change in Iran, he said: “Nobody knows.

web_results.title: U.S.-Israel launch another round of strikes on Iran following Khamenei's killing
web_results.description: President Trump said that "heavy and pinpoint bombing" of Iran would "continue, uninterrupted throughout the week or, as long as necessary."

web_results.title: Experts react: The US and Israel just unleashed a major attack on Iran. What’s next? - Atlantic Council
web_results.description: The question is, will the United States help the Iranian people chart a positive path forward, or will it leave them to the wolves after the bombs stop falling? ——Kelly J. Shannon is a visiting scholar at the Institute for Middle East Studies at George Washington University. She is also a member of the Atlantic Council’s Iran Strategy Project working group. Ten predictions for the potential US strikes on Iran · Iran’s regime is suffering from strategic vertigo. Its next misstep may be its last.

web_results.title: Six questions to answer about US-Israeli attack on Iran
web_results.description: Three US service members have been killed in action and five seriously injured as part of the operation in Iran, the US Central Command said on Sunday, without identifying the deceased. Trump warned of that possibility when announcing the strikes on Saturday, saying "the lives of courageous American heroes may be lost".

web_results.title: 2026 Israeli–United States strikes on Iran - Wikipedia
web_results.description: In retaliation, Iran launched dozens of its drones and ballistic missiles throughout the Persian Gulf in addition to targeting Israel as well as US military bases in Jordan, Kuwait, Bahrain, Qatar, Iraq, Saudi Arabia, and the United Arab Emirates. Additionally, Iran has reportedly launched strikes on civilian aviation facilities, including international airports in Kuwait and the UAE, and Iranian missiles were fired at British military bases in Cyprus. The retaliatory strikes have killed one civilian in Israel. Furthermore, the Yemen-based Houthis have announced that they will resume their attacks in the Red Sea.
